name: Google
metaDescription: Get interviews at Google: exact keywords recruiters filter on, sample resume bullets, and what the hiring bar looks like in 2026.
intro: Google receives huge volumes of applications every year. Standing out requires a clear, ATS-friendly resume that matches how they describe roles and what recruiters look for. This guide covers typical hiring expectations and resume strategies that align with Google's process.
hiringRequirements: Strong academic record and/or demonstrated impact in prior roles
Relevant experience that maps to the role's responsibilities and level
Evidence of leadership, collaboration, and structured problem-solving
Clear communication and ability to work in ambiguous, fast-paced environments
For technical roles: proficiency in relevant languages and systems; for non-technical: data fluency and stakeholder management
sampleSnippet: Led migration of core service to new platform, reducing p99 latency by 40% and improving reliability for 10M+ daily active users. Drove cross-functional alignment with product, SRE, and support teams and documented runbooks for on-call.
body:
Google recruiters and hiring managers look for impact, scope, and how you work with others. Use concrete metrics and avoid vague language. Match the level of the role—junior roles need evidence of growth and ownership; senior roles need system-level or org-level impact.

Keep formatting simple so ATS and human reviewers can parse your resume easily. Tailoring your resume to each specific job description at Google will improve your chances. WadeCV can help you reframe your experience to align with Google-style job posts and surface your strongest bullets.

faq.question: How do I tailor my resume for Google?
faq.answer: Match the language in the job description, emphasise impact with metrics (scale, users, performance), and show collaboration and ownership. Use clean formatting and avoid keyword stuffing.

name: McKinsey & Company
metaDescription: McKinsey resume format 2026: one-page pre-MBA structure, Personal Impact and Entrepreneurial Drive keywords, and what the PEI and screening round actually look for.
intro: McKinsey screens resumes against a specific set of Distinctive Traits — Personal Impact, Entrepreneurial Drive, Inclusive Leadership and Courageous Change — and a quantitative filter that expects nearly every bullet to have a number. The resume round is a binary gate before the Problem Solving Game (Solve) and the Personal Experience Interview (PEI). This guide covers exactly what partners, EMs and recruiters read for, how to structure the page for Associate, BA, Senior Associate, Engagement Manager and experienced hire tracks, and how to avoid the three reasons resumes get rejected at screen.
hiringRequirements: Outstanding academic record — top-decile GPA (3.7+ / First Class / 2:1 minimum at target schools) or exceptional performance at a non-target institution
Structured problem-solving demonstrated through case competitions, consulting clubs, student-led strategy projects, or prior consulting internships
Distinctive leadership with named role (President, VP, Captain, Founder) and measurable outcome — membership grown, revenue raised, policy changed
Entrepreneurial drive — a venture, non-profit, publication, patent, or self-initiated project that went beyond expectations
Quantified impact in every bullet — % saved, $ raised, users reached, team size led, weeks shaved
Evidence of inclusive collaboration across diverse teams (international, cross-functional, multi-stakeholder)
Communication fluency — published writing, public speaking, teaching, or presenting to senior audiences

Alignment with a specific practice or capability (Strategy & Corporate Finance, Operations, Digital/QuantumBlack, Implementation, Public & Social Sector, Healthcare, Risk) if targeting as an experienced hire
sampleSnippet: Led 4-person team in capstone engagement for Fortune 500 retailer; built market-entry model across 12 countries that identified $180M incremental revenue opportunity, adopted by CEO and rolled out over 18 months. Presented to board of directors and delivered 80-page final recommendation; synthesised insights from 40+ expert interviews and 200+ data sources.
body:
McKinsey resumes are one page for undergraduate, MBA and most experienced hires up to Engagement Manager — two pages are accepted only for Associate Partner level and above. The page structure partners expect is education first (degree, institution, classification, honours, relevant coursework, study-abroad), then professional experience reverse-chronologically, then leadership and extracurriculars, then additional (languages, certifications, publications, awards). Skills and interests sit at the very bottom and should be specific — 'French (native), Mandarin (professional working), Stata, SQL, Python' rather than 'Microsoft Office'.

Every bullet should answer three questions a screener asks in the six seconds they spend on your resume: What did you own, what did you do differently, and what measurable outcome did you produce? The canonical McKinsey bullet starts with a strong verb (Led, Built, Negotiated, Synthesised, Advised, Launched, Scaled), names the scope (team size, client revenue, geographies, data volume), describes the action (the analysis, model, experiment, or framework you applied), and closes with a quantified result ('reducing cost-to-serve by 22%', 'growing MAU 3.4x in eight months', 'delivering $12M in first-year run-rate savings').

Practice and capability signalling matters more than most candidates realise. Partners skim for fit with the hiring office's book of work — a Strategy & Corporate Finance partner in London reads differently from a QuantumBlack partner in New York or an Implementation partner in Chicago. If you are targeting a specific practice, mirror their language: 'zero-based budgeting', 'revenue growth management', 'operations diagnostic', 'advanced analytics', 'agile transformation', 'capability building'. A resume that reads like a generic 'strategy consultant' is less compelling than one that reads like a 'Operations consultant with a focus on supply-chain redesign for industrial clients'.

The three most common reasons McKinsey resumes get rejected at screen: (1) no quantification — bullets describing responsibilities rather than outcomes; (2) weak distinctive traits section — extracurriculars listed without a role title or outcome; (3) format that wastes space — long paragraph intros, wide margins, 12pt body text. Tailor to the target office and practice, quantify everything, and cut ruthlessly. WadeCV aligns your existing CV with McKinsey-style job descriptions, surfaces your most quantified bullets, and restructures the page to the one-page PEI-ready format. For an overview of how MBB screens differ from Big 4 consulting, see the [consulting resume pillar guide](/consulting-resume).

faq.question: How long should a McKinsey resume be?
faq.answer: One page for undergraduate applicants, MBAs, Associates and Senior Associates — non-negotiable. Engagement Manager and Associate Partner candidates may use one and a half to two pages if the content justifies it. Anything over two pages will be rejected at the CV screen regardless of the content. Use 10-11pt body text, 0.5-0.7 inch margins, and compact spacing to fit a 6-8 year career cleanly.

faq.question: What GPA do I need for McKinsey?
faq.answer: McKinsey does not publish a cut-off, but in practice resumes from target schools need a 3.7+ GPA or UK First Class / 2:1 to pass the automated screen. Non-target-school candidates need 3.8+ or a First, plus exceptional leadership, prior consulting internship, or Olympiad/competition results to compensate. If your GPA is below 3.5 / lower 2:1, list strong secondary signals (test scores, honours, distinctions, awards) before the GPA and consider adding 'Dean's List' or major-specific GPA if significantly higher.

faq.question: What keywords does McKinsey's resume screen look for?
faq.answer: The screen looks for evidence of McKinsey's Distinctive Traits. 'Led', 'built', 'founded', 'launched', 'grew', 'scaled', 'negotiated', 'advised', 'synthesised' signal Personal Impact and Entrepreneurial Drive. Named leadership titles ('President of X', 'Founder of Y', 'Captain of Z') signal Inclusive Leadership. Practice-specific terms ('operating model', 'growth strategy', 'due diligence', 'go-to-market', 'cost transformation', 'digital transformation') signal Courageous Change. Include numbers in almost every bullet — McKinsey's internal heuristic is that a strong resume has 80%+ of bullets quantified.

faq.question: Do I need an MBA to work at McKinsey?
faq.answer: No. McKinsey hires three main tracks: undergraduate (Business Analyst), advanced degree (Associate for MBA, PhD, JD, MD), and experienced hire (various titles including Associate, Senior Associate, Engagement Manager, depending on prior years of work). Experienced hires from industry, government, non-profits, and the military are common and often fast-tracked. Your resume must emphasise the functional expertise or industry depth that the hiring office is recruiting for.

faq.question: How do I tailor my resume for a specific McKinsey practice?
faq.answer: Review the McKinsey Insights page for the practice you are targeting (Strategy, Operations, Digital/QuantumBlack, Implementation, Sustainability, Healthcare, Marketing & Sales, Public Sector, Risk). Note the vocabulary, the frameworks, and the industries featured. Rewrite your bullets to use that vocabulary when honestly applicable — 'led operating model redesign', 'delivered zero-based budgeting wave', 'built ML model for churn prediction', 'ran change programme for 800 FTEs'. Move the most relevant experiences to the top of your professional experience section even if they are not the most recent.

faq.question: Should I include consulting clubs and case competitions on my McKinsey resume?
faq.answer: Yes — especially for undergraduate and MBA applicants, case competitions, consulting clubs, and student-led strategy projects are primary signals of consulting fit. List them under Leadership & Extracurriculars with a role title, measurable outcome, and a consulting-relevant verb. 'Won 1st place in 78-team Cornell Consulting Competition; developed go-to-market strategy for pre-seed fintech client adopted for $2M seed round' is stronger than 'Participated in consulting club'.

faq.question: What does the McKinsey Personal Experience Interview (PEI) resume bar look like?
faq.answer: The PEI explicitly asks candidates to tell stories against Personal Impact, Entrepreneurial Drive, Inclusive Leadership, and Courageous Change. If your resume doesn't contain at least one bullet that clearly maps to each of those traits, your candidacy is weaker. Before you submit, confirm you have: (1) a story of measurable impact with a named outcome, (2) a story of going beyond the scope / entrepreneurial initiative, (3) a story of leading or bringing along a diverse group, and (4) a story of driving change through resistance. Each story should appear as a bullet on your resume and be ready to expand to 4-6 minutes in interview.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons McKinsey rejects a resume?
faq.answer: Three patterns dominate: (1) No numbers — bullets describe responsibilities ('managed a team', 'analysed data', 'presented findings') without outcomes. (2) Format waste — long paragraph intros, large fonts, wide margins, leaving half the page blank. (3) Generic consulting language that signals no specific practice fit. Fix these and a previously-rejected resume can pass screen. WadeCV runs your CV through a McKinsey job description, quantifies bullets with real numbers from your history, and restructures the page to MBB one-page format.

commonMistakes: Using responsibility-based bullets ('responsible for', 'managed', 'oversaw') instead of outcome-based ones with numbers
Leaving GPA off the resume when it is above 3.7 — recruiters assume a missing GPA is below their screen threshold
Listing generic extracurriculars without a role title and outcome ('member of consulting club' vs 'President, Cornell Consulting Club — grew membership from 40 to 180')
Two-page resumes for undergraduate and MBA applicants — automatic rejection at most offices
Using 'McKinsey-style' language copied from the website without evidence to back it up
Omitting language proficiency levels (native / fluent / professional / conversational) when applying to non-US offices where bilingualism is required
Burying the most relevant practice experience at the bottom because it isn't the most recent
Generic skills section ('Microsoft Office, teamwork, leadership') — use specific tools (Stata, Alteryx, Tableau, PowerPoint mastery) and named languages

name: Goldman Sachs
metaDescription: Goldman Sachs resume format 2026: one-page banker structure, deal-experience bullets, division keywords (M&A, LevFin, ECM, Markets), and what the Super Day + HireVue screen actually evaluate.
intro: Goldman Sachs screens resumes against a bar partners describe as 'commercial intellect plus rigour' — a combination of GPA, quantified deal experience, and a visible 'spike' (Olympiad, D1 athletics, founded venture, published research). The CV screen feeds into HireVue video interview, Pymetrics cognitive assessment, and Super Day (3-6 back-to-back 30-minute behavioural + technical interviews). This guide covers exactly how Goldman partners read a resume, how to signal fit with Investment Banking vs Markets vs Asset Management vs Engineering, and what to include in your one-page CV to pass the screen.
hiringRequirements: Top-decile academic record — 3.5+ GPA / First / 2:1 minimum at targets (Wharton, Stern, Harvard, Columbia, LSE, Oxbridge, Bocconi, INSEAD), 3.7+ at semi-targets and non-targets
Standardised test scores if strong — SAT 1450+, ACT 32+, GMAT 730+, GRE 330+ — listed in the education block
Quantified deal experience for IB applicants — $ size, buyer/target/sponsor, your specific workstream, deal outcome (closed, announced, withdrawn)
Named leadership with a role title, team size, and quantified outcome — investment-club PM, student-government president, founded venture, D1 captain
Visible 'spike' in the additional section — Olympiad medal, published research, patent, sustained athletic, musical, or entrepreneurial achievement
Division signalling — IB (M&A, LevFin, ECM, DCM, Sponsors, Industry Groups), GS Asset Management, Global Markets (FICC, Equities), Goldman Sachs Research, GS Engineering, GS Marquee

Technical fluencies for the skills section — advanced Excel (three-statement, LBO, DCF, M&A accretion/dilution), PowerPoint, Capital IQ, FactSet, Bloomberg; Python and VBA for Markets / Engineering roles
Language proficiency relevant to the hiring office — French/German/Italian/Spanish for London-EU coverage, Mandarin for Hong Kong, Portuguese/Spanish for Sao Paulo
sampleSnippet: Built three-statement operating model, DCF, and precedent-transaction analysis for $1.4B Goldman Sachs sell-side advisory of specialty-chemicals target to European strategic acquirer; drafted 62-page confidential information memorandum and 40-page management presentation; deal signed at 11.8x LTM EBITDA, 22% premium to sponsor's entry, closing Q3 2025.
body:
Goldman Sachs resumes are one page for every Summer Analyst, Full-Time Analyst, Lateral Associate, and Experienced Associate application — no exceptions below Vice President. The partner or VP reviewing your CV spends under 45 seconds on the first read. Structure: education first (institution, degree, GPA to two decimals, classification, relevant coursework, study abroad, SAT/ACT/GMAT/GRE if strong), then professional experience reverse-chronologically with deal experience integrated or broken out, then leadership and extracurriculars, then additional (languages, technical skills, certifications, publications, awards). The additional section is where the 'spike' lives — Olympiad medals, patents, D1 athletics, published research, sustained entrepreneurial outcomes.

Goldman bullets are more precise than peers. Where Morgan Stanley bullets lean 'strategic' and JP Morgan bullets lean 'scale', Goldman bullets favour 'rigour' and 'commercial impact' — specific model names, specific memo deliverables, specific financing or transaction outcomes. The best Goldman IB bullets read like internal deal-team-memo summaries: '$ deal size, buyer/target/sponsor, your specific model or memo deliverable, deal outcome (closed, announced, withdrawn), and the relevant multiple or margin'. For Markets applicants, bullets should state asset class, client-coverage scope (top-N client names, AUM covered), risk metrics (VaR, DV01, delta exposure), and PnL contribution with Greenwich Coalition rankings where available.

Goldman organises Investment Banking by industry group (TMT, Healthcare, FIG, Natural Resources, Industrials, Consumer/Retail, Real Estate) and product group (M&A, LevFin, ECM, DCM, Sponsors). Your CV should signal the industry or product you are targeting — 'TMT sell-side M&A', 'FIG ECM', 'Healthcare LevFin', 'Sponsors coverage' — in the headline and in the most recent deal bullets. Markets organises by FICC (Rates, Credit, Mortgages, FX, Commodities) and Equities (Cash, Derivatives, Prime, Futures, One Delta) — name the specific desk, asset class, and client segment. GS Asset Management, GSAM Alternatives, and Goldman Sachs Research have their own CV norms — more CFA-weighted, research-note-weighted, and sector-coverage-weighted.

The four common resume-screen rejections at Goldman: (1) deal bullets without $ size, counterparty, or your specific workstream — generic 'supported M&A process' is a screen-kill phrase; (2) missing GPA when above 3.5 — screeners interpret absence as sub-threshold; (3) weak 'spike' — membership-level involvement in societies listed without role title, portfolio, or outcome; (4) two-page resume at Analyst or Associate level. Fix these and pass the HireVue + Super Day gate. WadeCV aligns your existing CV with a specific Goldman Sachs job description, rewrites bullets with quantified deal outcomes from your history, and reformats to Goldman's one-page standard. For cross-bank comparison and the differences with JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Citi, BlackRock, and elite boutiques, see the [investment banking resume pillar guide](/investment-banking-resume).

faq.question: How long should a Goldman Sachs resume be?
faq.answer: One page for every Summer Analyst, Full-Time Analyst, Lateral Associate, and Experienced Associate application. Vice President and above applying as experienced hires may extend to one and a half pages only if the substance demands it. Two pages are accepted only for MD-level or Partner-level lateral applications with 15+ years of named deal experience. Partners and VPs reviewing a Goldman CV spend under 45 seconds on the first read — a two-page CV at junior level loses the reader. Use 10-11pt body text and 0.5-inch margins and cut ruthlessly.

faq.question: What GPA does Goldman Sachs require?
faq.answer: Goldman does not publish a cut-off, but competitive resumes from target schools (Wharton, Stern, Harvard, Columbia, Ross, Mendoza, Haas, Johnson, LSE, Oxbridge, Bocconi, INSEAD, HEC) have 3.5+ GPA / First Class / 2:1 minimum. Semi-target and non-target applicants need 3.7+ plus a differentiating signal (winning stock-pitch, investment-club PM role, published research, early PE internship, quant competition medal). Do not hide your GPA — screeners assume absence means sub-3.5. If your cumulative is lower but your finance-major or quantitative-major GPA is higher, list both. Standardised test scores (SAT 1450+, ACT 32+, GMAT 730+, GRE 330+) strengthen the academic signal — list them.

faq.question: What is Goldman's HireVue + Pymetrics + Super Day and how does the resume feed into it?
faq.answer: HireVue is Goldman's asynchronous video interview — typically 3-5 questions, 30-60 seconds each, recorded and AI-scored. Pymetrics is a cognitive and behavioural assessment — 12 short games measuring attention, risk tolerance, pattern recognition, and emotional processing. Both feed into the Super Day invite decision along with the resume. Super Day is 3-6 back-to-back 30-minute in-person (or Zoom) behavioural + technical interviews. The resume remains the gatekeeper: HireVue and Pymetrics do not rescue a weak CV. Your resume needs to pass the screener before HireVue is unlocked, and a strong HireVue will not offset a weak resume.

faq.question: How should I tailor my resume to a specific Goldman division?
faq.answer: Read the division page (Goldman Sachs Investment Banking, Goldman Sachs Global Markets, Goldman Sachs Asset Management, Goldman Sachs Research, Goldman Sachs Engineering, Goldman Sachs Marquee) and mirror the vocabulary of the target division. IB Analyst: deal size, buyer/target/sponsor, your specific workstream. Markets: asset class, desk, client-coverage scope, risk metrics, PnL contribution. GSAM / Research: coverage universe, AUM, benchmark, alpha. Engineering: systems built, production scale, latency, reliability metrics. Industry-group CVs (TMT, FIG, Healthcare, Natural Resources) should lead with sector-relevant deal experience even if not the most recent role.

faq.question: What keywords should I use on a Goldman Sachs resume?
faq.answer: Deal-experience verbs that signal ownership: built, led, structured, negotiated, authored, drafted, priced. Outcome-type language: 'deal signed at $X size', 'financing commitment $Y at SOFR+Zbps', 'priced $A IPO at B% above range with C% first-day pop'. Division vocabulary — 'sell-side M&A', 'sponsor LBO', 'convertible issuance', 'investment-grade DCM', 'high-yield roadshow', 'rates flow', 'long-only coverage'. Named model and memo deliverables signal fluency: 'three-statement operating model', 'LBO capital-structure waterfall', 'M&A accretion/dilution', 'precedent-transaction analysis', 'management presentation', 'confidential information memorandum', 'fairness opinion'. Named tools for skills: advanced Excel, PowerPoint, Capital IQ, FactSet, Bloomberg, VBA, Python, Tableau, Marquee.

faq.question: How is a Goldman Sachs resume different from JP Morgan or Morgan Stanley?
faq.answer: Structurally identical — one page, education first, reverse-chronological experience, deal experience integrated or broken out, leadership, additional. The vocabulary and signal differ. Goldman bullets favour 'rigour', 'commercial impact', specific model names, specific memo deliverables. JP Morgan bullets favour 'scale', 'breadth', 'balance-sheet', universal-bank integration. Morgan Stanley bullets favour 'strategic', 'advisory depth', 'TMT dominance', Wealth-and-Asset-Management integration. Goldman weights 'spike' more heavily — Olympiad, D1 athletics, founded venture, published research — while JP Morgan weights breadth of high-quality experience and MS weights advisory judgement. If applying to all three, use the same base CV and swap vocabulary emphasis and most-recent deal experience.

faq.question: Can I get a Goldman Sachs interview from a non-target school?
faq.answer: Yes — Goldman increasingly recruits from non-targets if the CV signals finance or technical depth. The path is a winning stock-pitch competition (Cornell Stock Pitch, Ross Portfolio Competition, LSE, WISC), an investment-club PM role with a named portfolio and benchmark-comparable performance, a pre-IB internship at a regional bank or boutique, a sustained quant-research project with named methodology, or an Olympiad medal (Math, Physics, Chemistry, Informatics, Biology). Non-finance majors — Engineering, Physics, Math, CS, Statistics — are well-regarded at Markets desks, GS Engineering, GS Marquee, and TMT / Healthcare industry groups because of the quantitative rigour. Your CV must compensate for the non-target signal with a visible, specific, quantified finance or technical spike.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons Goldman Sachs rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) Deal bullets without $ size, counterparty, or your specific workstream — 'supported M&A process' is a screen-kill phrase. (2) Missing GPA when above 3.5 — screeners read absence as sub-threshold. (3) Weak 'spike' — extracurriculars listed without role title, duration, or measurable outcome. (4) Two-page resume at Analyst or Associate level — automatic screen reject. (5) Generic 'finance' framing when the role is division-specific (M&A vs LevFin vs ECM vs Markets vs GSAM vs Research vs Engineering). (6) Missing Series 79 / CFA progression on US bulge-bracket or asset-management applications. (7) Missing language proficiency for non-US office applications. (8) Skills section with generic tools rather than named fluencies. Unlike MBB where the additional section is where 'spikes' live, at Deloitte the additional section is typically where certifications, CPA/CFA progress, and specific software fluencies go — it reads more like a traditional corporate resume.

Bullets should lead with a strong verb, name the engagement or client type, describe the specific action, and close with an outcome. Deloitte's outcomes do not have to be MBB-grade numbers — 'supported X engagement, delivered Y output' is acceptable for early-career roles — but quantification still strengthens the CV. Great Deloitte bullets combine commercial language with delivery framing: 'Led SAP S/4HANA migration workstream for global industrial client; coordinated with 4 offshore developers, delivered configuration and integration testing to plan, enabling go-live on schedule with zero Severity-1 defects in first 30 days.'

Service-line signalling matters enormously at Deloitte. Monitor Deloitte (Strategy), Deloitte S&A (Strategy & Analytics), and Human Capital compete directly with MBB for top-tier strategy talent; your resume there should look like an MBB CV. Technology Consulting (SAP, Oracle, Salesforce, ServiceNow, AWS/Azure/GCP) wants implementation depth — system names, module names, go-live experience, user volumes, configuration versus custom-code ratios. Audit & Assurance wants accounting fundamentals: Revenue Recognition (ASC 606), Lease Accounting (ASC 842), SOX 404 testing, ICFR, PCAOB inspection experience. Tax wants technical depth in specific tax areas (Federal Corporate, State & Local, Transfer Pricing, M&A, International). Risk & Financial Advisory splits between Financial Advisory (M&A, valuation, forensic accounting) and Risk Advisory (cyber, controls, regulatory). Tailor the resume to the specific service line and offering.

Deloitte also recruits heavily for Deloitte Digital (design, UX, product, engineering) and Deloitte ConvergeHEALTH (life sciences analytics) — these units look more like tech companies than traditional consulting, and the CV should reflect that: portfolio links, shipped products, GitHub, certifications (AWS Certified, Google UX Design Professional). The common resume-screen rejections at Deloitte: (1) generic 'Big 4 consulting' framing when the role is a specific service line; (2) no certifications or certification progress for Audit, Tax, Risk roles; (3) unclear engagement scope on consulting bullets. faq.question: How long should a Deloitte resume be?
faq.answer: One page for undergraduate, MBA, and Senior Consultant applications. Manager and above can go to two pages if engagement history justifies it. For Audit, Tax, and Financial Advisory roles, a US-style 1-page resume is standard; for Consulting roles at MBA level in UK/Europe, a 2-page CV is acceptable if every bullet adds substance. Use 10-11pt body text and 0.5-inch margins; dense is better than padded.

faq.question: Is Deloitte Consulting as competitive as MBB?
faq.answer: Monitor Deloitte (the strategy arm from the 2013 Monitor Group acquisition) and Deloitte S&A (Strategy & Analytics) recruit at MBB levels and often interview the same MBA candidates. Other Deloitte Consulting offerings — Human Capital, Customer & Marketing, Operations Transformation, Technology Consulting — are less directly MBB-competitive but still selective. The resume expectations mirror the practice: MBB-grade bullets for Monitor/S&A, implementation-depth bullets for Tech Consulting, HR/org-design bullets for Human Capital.

faq.question: What certifications help for a Deloitte Audit resume?
faq.answer: CPA is effectively required for US Audit roles, though candidates can be hired pre-CPA if they commit to passing within 18 months. List CPA exam progress explicitly ('CPA: passed FAR, AUD, REG; scheduled BEC for Q2 2026'). CA / ACCA / ACA are the UK equivalents. For International Audit, CIA (Certified Internal Auditor) or CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) add value. Include ICFR, SOX 404, PCAOB inspection exposure, and specific accounting framework experience (ASC 606, ASC 842, IFRS 15, IFRS 16) on the CV.

faq.question: What does Deloitte Technology Consulting look for?
faq.answer: Implementation depth. Named systems: SAP (S/4HANA, Ariba, SuccessFactors), Oracle (Fusion, EBS, NetSuite), Salesforce (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud), ServiceNow (ITSM, HRSD, CSM), Workday, AWS / Azure / GCP cloud platforms. Implementation methodology fluency: Activate, SAP RUN, SAFe, Agile at scale. Configuration vs custom-code ratios matter — Deloitte Tech Consulting leans 'configuration-first'. List project codes: 'led SAP S/4HANA RISE greenfield go-live for 12,000-user industrial client, coordinated with 8-person Deloitte + 20-person SAP team'.

faq.question: How is Deloitte different from PwC, EY, and KPMG?
faq.answer: Structurally similar: all Big 4 firms have Audit, Tax, Advisory / Consulting, and Risk. Vocabulary differs. Deloitte uses 'offerings' and 'service lines'; PwC uses 'practice' and 'line of service'; EY uses 'service line'; KPMG uses 'function'. Deloitte is the largest globally with heaviest Consulting revenue share; PwC is #2 with Deals focus; EY is strong in Tax and M&A; KPMG leans Audit-heavy. Tailor keywords to the firm you are targeting but the resume structure stays the same.

faq.question: Can I move from Deloitte Audit to Deloitte Consulting?
faq.answer: Yes — internal mobility is a major Deloitte recruiting advantage. Audit Senior Associates routinely move to Consulting (Financial Advisory, Technology Consulting, Risk Advisory). Your CV for the internal move should emphasise client-delivery experience, industry focus, and any consulting-adjacent work (M&A due diligence, SOX implementation, ERP audit). List the Deloitte tenure as 'Audit Senior Associate, Deloitte & Touche LLP' with bullets showing client impact beyond audit fieldwork.

faq.question: What keywords should I use on a Deloitte resume?
faq.answer: Service-line specific. Consulting: 'operating model transformation', 'go-to-market strategy', 'customer experience redesign', 'organisation design', 'change management'. Technology: 'SAP implementation', 'Salesforce configuration', 'cloud migration', 'enterprise architecture', 'API integration'. Audit: 'SOX 404', 'ICFR', 'revenue recognition (ASC 606)', 'lease accounting (ASC 842)', 'PCAOB inspection readiness'. Tax: 'transfer pricing', 'FIN 48', 'SALT', 'international tax', 'M&A tax'. Risk: 'cyber', 'ISO 27001', 'NIST', 'third-party risk', 'regulatory compliance'.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons Deloitte rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) Generic 'Big 4 Advisory' framing when the role is a specific service line. (2) Missing certifications or certification progress for Audit, Tax, or Risk Advisory. (3) No client-delivery outcomes on consulting bullets — 'participated in engagement' is weak; 'delivered workstream that identified $X savings' is strong. name: BCG
metaDescription: BCG resume guide 2026: one-page MBB format, Boston Consulting Group keywords the screener looks for, and what Casey the AI and human partners actually screen for.
intro: BCG (Boston Consulting Group) uses a two-stage screen: Casey, a chatbot-led video and case-prep evaluator, and a partner-led CV review. The resume itself is read against four traits — Impact, Drive, People, Perspective — and a quantification bar that mirrors McKinsey's. This guide covers what BCG partners look for by region and practice, how to structure the page for Associate, Consultant, Project Leader and experienced hire tracks, and how to pass the resume round into the Casey Chatbot and case interview stages.
hiringRequirements: Top-decile academic record — 3.7+ GPA / First Class / 2:1 from target institution, or First / 3.8+ from non-target with standout secondary signals
Structured problem-solving demonstrated through case competitions, strategy projects, or quantitative research
Named leadership — President, VP, Captain, Founder — with measurable outcomes (membership grown, revenue raised, competition won)
Quantified impact in every bullet — percentages, dollars, users, team size, timeline
Distinct 'spike' — a patent, startup, Olympiad medal, publication, athletic achievement, or military record that marks you apart
Communication proficiency with senior audiences — teaching, public speaking, published writing, or presenting to executives
Language skills relevant to the hiring office (German, French, Spanish, Mandarin, Japanese, Portuguese — list level honestly)
Alignment with a BCG practice or unit (BCG X, Gamma, Platinion, Digital Ventures, Strategy, Operations, Social Impact, Public Sector, Industrial Goods, Healthcare, TMT)
sampleSnippet: Led 3-person workstream on growth strategy for FTSE 100 consumer client; built market-sizing model across 8 European markets and identified €250M addressable opportunity in D2C channel. Co-facilitated executive workshop with C-suite, drove consensus on €45M three-year investment plan, and contributed to 120-page final deliverable.
body:
BCG resumes are one page for all Associate, Consultant, and Project Leader applicants. Principals and above can extend to a page and a half only if the content demands it. Structure the page education first (degree, institution, classification, GPA, relevant coursework, exchange terms), then professional experience reverse-chronologically, then leadership and entrepreneurship, then additional information (languages with levels, certifications, publications, awards). The additional section at the bottom is where BCG partners look for your 'spike' — a distinguishing signal that separates you from other 3.8 GPA candidates.

Every bullet should open with a strong verb (Led, Built, Launched, Scaled, Negotiated, Designed, Advised), state the scope (team size, client revenue, geographies, data volume, duration), describe the action (the hypothesis you tested, the model you built, the pitch you ran), and close with a quantified outcome. BCG partners read for 'so what?' — if a bullet does not answer that question with a number, it gets skimmed. The canonical BCG bullet format: 'Led 4-person team on [engagement type] for [client description]; delivered [specific output] that [measurable outcome].' A bullet without a number is a bullet BCG will not count.

Practice and unit signalling is especially important for BCG because the firm is organised into named units with distinct recruiting pipelines. BCG X (the digital-build arm) recruits engineers, product managers and designers separately from Strategy. BCG Gamma (advanced analytics) looks for data scientists with ML-model-deployment experience. Platinion recruits enterprise architects and cyber experts. If you are targeting one of these units, mirror their vocabulary: 'model ops', 'MLOps', 'causal inference', 'LLM fine-tuning', 'cloud migration', 'zero-trust architecture'. Generic 'strategy consultant' framing is weaker than unit-specific framing.

Casey, the BCG chatbot, is deployed after the resume round and scores candidates on case-prep-adjacent signals. A strong resume alone does not secure an offer, but a weak resume disqualifies. The three most common resume rejections at BCG: (1) no quantified bullets, (2) generic rather than practice-specific language, (3) missing or weak spike in the additional-information section. WadeCV takes your CV, aligns it with a specific BCG job description, quantifies every bullet with real outcomes from your history, and reformats the page to BCG one-page conventions. For an overview of how BCG compares to McKinsey and Bain, see the [consulting resume pillar guide](/consulting-resume).

faq.question: How long should a BCG resume be?
faq.answer: One page. This is non-negotiable for Associate, Consultant, and Project Leader applications across every BCG office globally. Principals and Managing Directors applying as experienced hires may extend to one and a half pages if the substance justifies it. Use 10-11pt body text, 0.5-inch margins, and cut ruthlessly before going to a second page — screeners read the first page first and may never scroll to the second.

faq.question: What does Casey, the BCG chatbot, assess?
faq.answer: Casey is deployed after the resume screen but before the first case interview. It runs candidates through a structured conversation including a mini-case, behavioural questions, and sometimes a video response to a strategy prompt. The resume remains the gatekeeper — Casey does not rescue a weak resume. Your CV must pass the partner-led screen before you see Casey. Casey's existence doesn't change what BCG looks for on the resume: Impact, Drive, People, Perspective, each demonstrated with quantified bullets.

faq.question: Is BCG X or BCG Gamma easier to get into than BCG Strategy?
faq.answer: Not easier, just different. BCG X recruits engineers, PMs and designers with build-delivery experience — the bar there is 'has shipped production software to real users', not 'has case-competition wins'. BCG Gamma recruits data scientists with ML-model-deployment experience — the bar is 'has deployed ML models that moved a business KPI'. The resume format for these units mirrors tech-company resumes more than traditional MBB CVs: lead with impact, include a projects/publications section, list languages and frameworks with proficiency. Tailor accordingly.

faq.question: What GPA does BCG require?
faq.answer: BCG does not publish a cut-off, but target-school resumes need 3.7+ GPA / First Class / 2:1 minimum to pass automated screens. Non-target applicants need 3.8+ / First plus exceptional spike. If your GPA is below 3.5 / lower 2:1, emphasise strong test scores (GRE 330+, GMAT 730+), major GPA if higher, or a Master's degree with distinction. Do not hide your GPA — missing GPAs are assumed to be sub-threshold.

faq.question: What keywords should I include on a BCG resume?
faq.answer: Action verbs that signal Impact and Drive: led, built, launched, scaled, negotiated, founded, grew, drove, delivered. Practice-specific vocabulary when truthfully applicable: 'operating model', 'go-to-market', 'zero-based design', 'cost-to-serve', 'customer experience transformation', 'pricing strategy', 'post-merger integration'. Named tools: Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Alteryx, Salesforce, SAP. And outcome-type keywords: 'delivering $X savings', 'growing revenue by Y%', 'reducing cost by Z%', 'shortening time-to-market by W weeks'.

faq.question: How is a BCG resume different from a McKinsey resume?
faq.answer: Structurally almost identical: one page, education first, reverse chronological experience, leadership, additional. The differences are in vocabulary emphasis. BCG favours 'impact', 'growth', 'transformation', 'digital', 'advantage', 'value' — echoing its strategic-advantage branding. McKinsey favours 'hypothesis', 'synthesis', 'framework', 'insight', 'capability'. BCG also places heavier weight on the 'spike' in the additional section than McKinsey does. If you are applying to both, use the same content base and swap 5-8 keywords.

faq.question: Can I get a BCG interview as an experienced hire?
faq.answer: Yes — BCG recruits experienced hires into Associate, Consultant, Project Leader, and Principal roles depending on years and functional expertise. The bar is industry or functional depth (e.g. ten years in retail operations, or five years as a product manager who has shipped ML products), a quantified track record of impact, and evidence you can translate that expertise into client-facing consulting. Your resume must emphasise functional spikes — 'built SKU rationalisation framework adopted across 15 retailers' — rather than generic leadership.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons BCG rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) Bullets describing responsibilities ('owned X, managed Y') without quantified outcomes. (2) Two-page resumes for pre-Principal candidates — automatic screen reject. (3) No spike — an otherwise-solid 3.8 GPA resume with generic extracurriculars looks interchangeable with dozens of others. (4) Missing language proficiency for non-English-speaking office applications. (5) Generic 'consultant' framing when the role is a specific practice or unit (BCG X, Gamma, Platinion). WadeCV aligns your CV with a specific BCG job description, quantifies every bullet, and restructures the page to MBB one-page conventions.

Submitting a two-page resume for a Consultant or Associate role — automatic screen reject
Leaving a GPA off when it is above 3.7 — screeners interpret absence as sub-threshold
Missing or weak 'spike' in the additional-information section — no patent, Olympiad, startup, publication, athletic achievement, or distinctive award
Generic consulting language ('strategy consultant', 'client-facing') without unit-specific signals (BCG X, Gamma, Platinion, Strategy, Ops)

name: Bain & Company

intro: Bain & Company screens resumes against its True North values — Passion, Straight Talk, Outsider Outlook, Grit, Tugboat Spirit — and a heavy private-equity due-diligence bias given Bain's outsized PEG (Private Equity Group) practice. hiringRequirements: Top-decile academic record — 3.7+ GPA / First Class / 2:1 minimum at target schools, with a stretch to 3.8+ / First at non-targets
Evidence of 'grit' — sustained commitment to a pursuit over 3+ years with measurable progression (sport, music, research, venture, competitive programming)
Named leadership with a role title, team size, and quantified outcome
Commercial orientation — lemonade stand, side business, trading, or P&L responsibility beats theoretical strategy coursework

body:
Bain resumes are one page for all undergraduate, MBA, and experienced-hire Associate Consultant through Consultant roles — the partner reviewing your CV expects to read the entire document in under 60 seconds. Structure: education first (institution, degree, classification, GPA, relevant coursework, study abroad), then professional experience reverse-chronologically, then leadership and extracurriculars, then additional (languages with proficiency levels, technical skills, publications, awards). The additional section is where Bain partners look for 'grit' and 'outsider outlook' — a sustained commitment that separates you from other 3.8 GPA candidates.

Bain bullets are notably more commercial than McKinsey's. Where McKinsey emphasises 'hypothesis-driven synthesis' and 'framework application', Bain bullets favour 'commercial impact', 'EBITDA', 'value creation', 'due diligence', 'investment thesis', 'growth opportunity'. The best Bain bullets sound like they belong in a Private Equity investment committee memo: 'Identified £X EBITDA upside from pricing optimisation in commodity categories; quantified by regression analysis of 2 years of SKU-level sales data across 400 stores.' Every bullet should lead with a strong verb, state scope, describe the action, and close with a dollar-or-percent outcome.

Bain's Private Equity Group (PEG) is the largest commercial due-diligence practice in consulting — around 30% of Bain's revenue and a major recruiting pipeline. If you are targeting PEG (or any Bain office with a strong PEG practice, especially London, Boston, Chicago, New York), your resume should signal commercial due-diligence readiness: exposure to investor memos, LBO modelling, commercial DD frameworks (market size, growth drivers, competitive position, value-creation levers), and an ability to deliver findings to time pressure. Non-PEG practices (Customer Experience, Digital/Vector, Operations, Advanced Analytics, Private Equity, Performance Improvement) have their own keyword sets — mirror the language of the practice you are targeting.

The three common resume-screen rejections at Bain: (1) no commercial quantification — bullets that describe strategy projects without a dollar/percent outcome; (2) no 'grit' signal — extracurriculars listed without sustained commitment or progression; (3) two-page resume or fonts below 10pt. Fix these and pass screen. WadeCV aligns your existing CV with a Bain job description, rewrites bullets with quantified commercial outcomes, and reformats the page to Bain's one-page standard. faq.question: How long should a Bain resume be?
faq.answer: One page for all Associate Consultant, Consultant, and Case Team Leader applications. Managers and above applying as experienced hires may extend to one and a half pages only if the substance demands it. Partners reviewing a Bain CV spend under 60 seconds on the first read — a two-page CV loses the reader. Use 10-11pt body text and 0.5-inch margins and cut ruthlessly.

faq.question: What is Bain's Sova assessment and how does the resume feed into it?
faq.answer: Sova is Bain's online psychometric assessment — a combination of situational judgement, numerical, verbal reasoning and personality inventory. It is administered after the resume screen in many Bain offices. The resume remains the gatekeeper: Sova does not rescue a weak CV. Your CV needs to pass partner screening before Sova is unlocked. Sova scores are combined with resume screen and first-round interview performance to make the decision-day call.

faq.question: Does Bain PEG (Private Equity Group) recruit differently?
faq.answer: Yes, PEG has its own recruiting pipeline in many offices, with the CV evaluated by PEG partners and managers. PEG CVs emphasise commercial-due-diligence-ready signals: exposure to investor memos, LBO / commercial DD modelling, time-pressured delivery, and commercial rather than strategy framing. If you are targeting PEG, include any private equity, investment banking, corporate development, or transaction-advisory experience prominently and use PEG vocabulary: 'value-creation plan', 'commercial due diligence', 'market-growth build-up', 'red-flag issues', 'synergy quantification'.

faq.question: What GPA does Bain require?
faq.answer: Bain does not publish a cut-off, but competitive resumes from target schools have a 3.7+ GPA / First / 2:1. Non-target applicants need 3.8+ / First and either strong standardised test scores (GMAT 730+, GRE 330+) or an unusual spike (Olympiad, D1 athletics, published research, venture exit). Do not hide your GPA — a missing GPA is assumed sub-threshold. If your GPA is mediocre but your major GPA is higher, list both.

faq.question: What keywords should I use on a Bain resume?
faq.answer: Commercial and outcome-focused: 'delivered £X EBITDA', 'identified Y% margin upside', 'grew revenue by Z%', 'accelerated time-to-value by W months'. Practice-specific terms: 'commercial due diligence', 'value-creation plan', 'post-merger integration', 'pricing optimisation', 'customer experience redesign', 'operating model'. Action verbs that signal grit and ownership: led, built, scaled, launched, negotiated, drove, delivered, founded. faq.question: How is a Bain resume different from McKinsey or BCG?
faq.answer: Structurally identical — one page, education first, reverse-chronological experience, leadership, additional. The vocabulary differs. McKinsey favours 'hypothesis', 'synthesis', 'framework', 'insight'. BCG favours 'impact', 'growth', 'transformation', 'advantage'. Bain favours 'results', 'commercial', 'value creation', 'EBITDA', 'due diligence'. Bain also weights 'grit' more heavily — a sustained 3-5 year commitment to something difficult (athletics, music, research, venture) is a strong Bain signal. If applying to all three, use the same base and swap vocabulary emphasis.

faq.question: Can I get a Bain interview as an experienced hire?
faq.answer: Yes. Bain recruits experienced hires into Associate Consultant, Consultant, Case Team Leader, and Manager roles depending on years and functional expertise. Functional depth matters most: five years in commercial strategy at a Fortune 500, three years at a top-tier PE fund, or deep vertical expertise (healthcare payer strategy, retail pricing, PE value creation) outperforms generic consulting experience. Your CV must signal industry or functional depth — 'led pricing programme that delivered £40M EBITDA uplift for national supermarket' — rather than generic leadership.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons Bain rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) No commercial quantification — bullets describing strategy without dollar, percent, or time outcomes. (2) Weak 'grit' signal — extracurriculars without sustained commitment. body:
JP Morgan resumes are one page for every Summer Analyst, Full-Time Analyst, Lateral Associate, and Experienced Associate application across the Corporate & Investment Bank, Commercial Bank, and Asset & Wealth Management divisions. Structure: education first (institution, degree, GPA to two decimals, classification, relevant coursework, study abroad, SAT/ACT/GMAT/GRE if strong), then professional experience reverse-chronologically with deal experience integrated or broken out, then leadership, then additional (languages, technical skills, certifications, publications). The additional section at JP Morgan is read for breadth and quality — multiple named leadership roles with outcomes outweigh a single narrow extracurricular.

JP Morgan bullets lean 'scale' and 'balance-sheet'. Where Goldman bullets favour 'rigour' and Morgan Stanley bullets favour 'advisory depth', JP Morgan bullets often showcase the universal-bank franchise — multi-product client relationships, balance-sheet-enabled financing, and cross-division integration. faq.question: Should I list CFA progression on a Morgan Stanley resume?
faq.answer: Depends on the division. For MSIM (Morgan Stanley Investment Management) — yes, CFA Level 1 or above is a strong differentiator and should be in the additional section. For Institutional Equities research and sales — yes, weighted. For Wealth Management — Series 7 / 63 / 65 / 66 / CFP are more weighted than CFA, though CFA helps for ultra-high-net-worth coverage and MSWM Research. For pure IB Analyst applications — CFA Level 1 is a mild plus (signals finance commitment) but not expected. Always list progression ('CFA Level 2 Candidate, June 2026 sitting') rather than aspirational (never list CFA unless you have passed at least Level 1).

faq.question: Can I get a Morgan Stanley interview from a non-target school?
faq.answer: Yes — MS recruits from non-targets if the CV signals advisory depth or deep domain expertise. The path is a winning case competition (Ross, Wharton, Darden, LSE), a published research note on a tech or healthcare sub-sector, an investment-club PM role with a named portfolio and benchmark performance, a pre-IB internship at a regional bank or boutique (especially TMT or Healthcare focused), or a sustained athletic, research, or entrepreneurial outcome. Non-finance majors — Engineering, CS, Biology — are well-regarded for TMT and Healthcare industry groups. Your CV must compensate for the non-target signal with a visible, specific, quantified advisory or domain spike.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons Morgan Stanley rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) Deal bullets without $ size, counterparty, or your specific advisory workstream — generic 'supported M&A' is a screen-kill phrase. (2) Missing GPA when above 3.5 — screeners assume sub-threshold. (3) Weak advisory-judgement signal — bullets listing quantitative model work without any client-facing or memo-authorship language. (4) Two-page resume at Analyst or Associate level — automatic screen reject. (5) Wrong-division framing — applying to Wealth Management with IB-only bullets, or to MSIM with generic advisory framing. (6) Missing Series 79 / CFA / Series 7-63-65-66 / CFP progression where relevant to the specific track. (7) Missing language proficiency for non-US office applications. (8) Additional section with generic skills ('Microsoft Office', 'teamwork') instead of named fluencies. WadeCV aligns your CV with a specific Morgan Stanley job description, rewrites bullets with quantified outcomes, and reformats to MS's one-page standard.

name: BlackRock
metaDescription: BlackRock resume format 2026: one-page asset-management structure, investment-analyst bullets, division keywords (Fundamental Equities, Systematic, Fixed Income, Alternatives, Aladdin), and what the Super Day + HireVue screen actually evaluate.
intro: BlackRock is the largest asset manager globally with over $10T AUM across active and index, the Aladdin risk platform running approximately 10% of global investable assets, and a private-markets arm (BlackRock Alternatives, GIP, HPS-integrated, Preqin-powered) that is the firm's fastest-growing segment. Recruiters screen CVs against a bar that weights CFA progression, sustained investment-research signal, Aladdin fluency, and quantitative rigour alongside standard advisory or markets experience. The CV screen feeds into HireVue video interview, Pymetrics cognitive assessment, and Super Day. This guide covers exactly how BlackRock recruiters read a resume, how to signal fit with Fundamental Equities vs Systematic vs Fixed Income vs Multi-Asset vs Alternatives vs Aladdin vs Index, and what to include in your one-page CV to pass the screen.
hiringRequirements: Strong academic record — 3.5+ GPA / First / 2:1 minimum at targets (Wharton, Stern, Harvard, MIT, Princeton, Chicago, LSE, Oxbridge, Bocconi, INSEAD), 3.7+ at semi-targets and non-targets; quantitative majors (Math, Physics, CS, Statistics, Engineering) strongly weighted for Systematic and Aladdin roles
Standardised test scores if strong — SAT 1450+, GRE 330+, GMAT 730+ — listed in the education block
CFA progression — Level 1 passed for Fundamental Equities, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset, and research applications; Level 2 candidate status is a strong differentiator

Investment-research signal — a published research note (student-managed investment fund, investment-club PM, case-competition-winning write-up, university-endowment research project, stock-pitch competition entry)
Quantified coverage experience — AUM covered, sector or universe size, benchmark, alpha or benchmark-comparable performance

Division signalling — Fundamental Equities, Systematic, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset Strategies and Solutions (MASS), Alternatives (BlackRock Alternatives, GIP, HPS, Preqin), Index, Aladdin, BlackRock Technology
Technical fluencies — Aladdin (or willingness to learn), advanced Excel, Python, R, SQL, Bloomberg, FactSet; VBA, MATLAB for Systematic and Aladdin engineering
Language proficiency relevant to the hiring office — French/German/Italian/Spanish for London-EU, Mandarin for Hong Kong, Japanese for Tokyo, Portuguese for Sao Paulo
sampleSnippet: Covered 24-name large-cap US software universe for BlackRock Fundamental Equities long-only mandate ($18B AUM); built three-year revenue-and-margin build-up models with AI-capex layer; maintained comp set; authored 11 quarterly research notes; three high-conviction buys added at combined 340bps portfolio weight, contributing 120bps alpha vs Russell 1000 Growth over 12 months.
body:
BlackRock resumes are one page for every Summer Analyst, Full-Time Analyst, Lateral Associate, and Experienced Associate application across Fundamental Equities, Systematic, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset Strategies and Solutions, Alternatives (BlackRock Alternatives, GIP, HPS-integrated, Preqin), Index, Aladdin, and BlackRock Technology. Structure: education first (institution, degree, GPA to two decimals, classification, relevant coursework, study abroad, SAT/ACT/GRE/GMAT if strong), then professional experience reverse-chronologically with investment-research experience integrated or broken out, then leadership and investment-research extracurriculars, then additional (languages, technical skills including Aladdin fluency, CFA progression, publications, awards). CFA progression should move to a prominent position in the additional section — at BlackRock it is weighted more than at IB-only peers.

BlackRock bullets are notably different from IB bullets. Where Goldman IB bullets favour 'rigour' and JP Morgan IB bullets favour 'scale', BlackRock bullets favour 'investment-research signal', 'quantified coverage', 'AUM', 'alpha', and 'benchmark'. The best BlackRock Investment Analyst bullet reads like a sleeve PM's portfolio-review summary: 'coverage universe (number of names, sector, AUM), your specific research output (models built, notes authored, theses pitched), portfolio impact (position sizing, weight, alpha or benchmark-comparable performance)'. For Fundamental Equities applicants: name the sector, the AUM, the benchmark (Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 2000 Value, MSCI ACWI, S&P 500), and alpha or benchmark-comparable performance. For Systematic and Aladdin Engineering applicants: factor models built, backtest Sharpe, production throughput, risk-model coverage, named portfolio-construction tools (Aladdin PM, Aladdin Risk, Aladdin for Wealth). For Alternatives applicants (BlackRock Alternatives, GIP, HPS-integrated, Preqin): deal diligence, investment-committee memos authored, named portfolio companies, fund-level IRRs, capital raised.

BlackRock organises its investment platforms by asset class and methodology: Fundamental Equities (active stock-picking), Systematic (quant factor-based), Fixed Income (active and passive across global rates, credit, securitised, municipals), Multi-Asset Strategies and Solutions (MASS — customised solutions for insurance, pension, and sovereign clients), Alternatives (BlackRock Alternatives, including the newly integrated GIP infrastructure platform, HPS private credit, and Preqin data platform), Index (iShares ETFs, institutional index), Aladdin (risk platform run for internal and external clients), and BlackRock Technology (Aladdin Wealth, Aladdin for ETFs, Aladdin Sustainability). Your CV should signal the specific platform — 'Fundamental Equities US Large-Cap Growth', 'Systematic Long/Short Equity', 'Fixed Income Global Rates', 'MASS Insurance Solutions', 'BlackRock Alternatives Private Credit', 'GIP Infrastructure', 'iShares Product Development', 'Aladdin Portfolio Management'. The Aladdin and BlackRock Technology tracks are engineering-heavy — CVs should emphasise systems built, production throughput, latency, and named clients served.

The four common resume-screen rejections at BlackRock: (1) missing CFA progression on Fundamental Equities, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset, or Alternatives applications — screeners expect at least Level 1 candidate; (2) missing investment-research signal — no published note, no student-managed investment fund involvement, no stock-pitch competition, no investment-club PM role; (3) generic 'finance' framing when the role is Fundamental vs Systematic vs Index vs Alternatives vs Aladdin (each has distinct vocabulary); (4) two-page resume at Analyst or Associate level. Fix these and pass the HireVue + Super Day gate. WadeCV aligns your existing CV with a specific BlackRock job description, rewrites bullets with quantified research and portfolio outcomes from your history, and reformats to BlackRock's one-page standard. For cross-firm comparison and the differences with Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Citi, and elite boutiques, see the [investment banking resume pillar guide](/investment-banking-resume).

faq.question: How long should a BlackRock resume be?
faq.answer: One page for every Summer Analyst, Full-Time Analyst, Lateral Associate, and Experienced Associate application across Fundamental Equities, Systematic, Fixed Income, MASS, Alternatives, Index, Aladdin, and BlackRock Technology. Vice President and above applying as experienced hires may extend to one and a half pages only if the substance demands it. Two pages are accepted only for Managing Director lateral applications with 15+ years of named investment or infrastructure experience. The PM or VP reviewing your CV spends under 45 seconds — a two-page CV at junior level loses the reader. Use 10-11pt body text and 0.5-inch margins.

faq.question: How important is CFA progression on a BlackRock resume?
faq.answer: Very important for Fundamental Equities, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset (MASS), and Alternatives applications — CFA Level 1 passed or Level 2 candidate status is a strong differentiator, particularly for non-target school applicants. For Systematic and Aladdin Engineering roles, CFA is less weighted than a quantitative Master's, published research, or Kaggle / ML-competition signal. For iShares Product Development and Index roles, CFA Level 1 is a mild plus. Always list progression honestly — 'CFA Level 2 Candidate, August 2026 sitting' is credible; listing CFA without having passed any level is disqualifying. BlackRock recruiters verify with the CFA Institute.

faq.question: What investment-research signals does BlackRock look for?
faq.answer: A published research note is the strongest — a student-managed investment fund write-up (Wharton Investment and Trading Group, Michigan MIIF, Notre Dame Student Investment Fund, LSE Alternative Investments Conference, Oxford Investment Research Society), a winning stock-pitch competition (Cornell Stock Pitch, Pan-Asia Investment Banking Conference, CFA Institute Research Challenge), a university-endowment research project, or a published industry note on a firm's website. Second-strongest is a sustained investment-club PM role with a named portfolio, benchmark, and benchmark-comparable performance. Third is a pre-AM internship at an asset manager (BlackRock, Vanguard, Fidelity, T. Rowe Price, Wellington, Capital Group, AllianceBernstein, MFS, Invesco, State Street Global Advisors). List any of these with named metrics — portfolio size, benchmark, alpha, position count, thesis accuracy.

faq.question: How do I signal fit with BlackRock Fundamental Equities vs Systematic?
faq.answer: Fundamental Equities CVs emphasise qualitative research, management-team assessment, sector-coverage depth, three-statement modelling, and written research notes. Name the sector, the AUM, the benchmark, and any named PM you worked under. Systematic CVs emphasise factor-model construction, backtesting, out-of-sample Sharpe, risk-model coverage, production throughput, and named portfolio-construction tools (Aladdin PM, Aladdin Risk). Quantitative majors (Math, Physics, Statistics, CS, Engineering), Master's in Financial Engineering / Math Finance / Computational Finance, Kaggle placement, published quantitative research, or quantitative-finance competition win are heavily weighted for Systematic. The two tracks have distinct vocabulary — do not cross-apply without rewriting.

faq.question: What is BlackRock Alternatives and how should my CV reflect it?
faq.answer: BlackRock Alternatives is the private-markets arm covering private equity, private credit, real estate, infrastructure (with the recent integration of GIP — Global Infrastructure Partners), hedge fund solutions, and the HPS Investment Partners private-credit business (also recently integrated). The Preqin data platform integration extends private-markets data coverage. BlackRock Alternatives CVs should emphasise: named deal diligence, investment-committee memos authored, LBO or DCF modelling for private-markets-specific structures (waterfall, carry, DPI, TVPI, IRR), named portfolio companies or infrastructure assets, and any sponsor-coverage or secondaries experience. CFA Level 1 minimum; CFA Level 2 strongly preferred. For GIP applicants: infrastructure-specific signal (project-finance modelling, PPP structuring, energy-transition experience). For HPS applicants: private-credit-specific signal (unitranche, second-lien, direct lending, distressed).

faq.question: What is Aladdin and how do I signal fluency on my CV?
faq.answer: Aladdin (Asset Liability Debt and Derivative Investment Network) is BlackRock's risk, portfolio management, and operations platform, used internally across BlackRock and licensed to approximately 200 external clients running ~10% of global investable assets. Aladdin fluency is a strong differentiator for any BlackRock application. If you have used Aladdin (common at many institutional clients, university endowments, and some pension funds), name it explicitly: 'Aladdin PM (portfolio management)', 'Aladdin Risk', 'Aladdin Wealth', 'Aladdin for ETFs', 'Aladdin Sustainability', 'eFront'. If you have not used Aladdin, list any comparable risk or portfolio-analytics platform (FactSet PA, Barra One, Bloomberg PORT, MSCI RiskMetrics, Charles River, SimCorp) — recruiters read familiarity with peer platforms as transferable. Aladdin Engineering applicants should list specific Aladdin modules built or maintained.

faq.question: Can I get a BlackRock interview from a non-target school?
faq.answer: Yes — BlackRock actively recruits from non-targets if the CV signals investment-research depth. The path is a winning research-challenge entry (CFA Institute Research Challenge), a published note, a student-managed investment-fund PM role with benchmark-comparable performance, a pre-AM internship at a regional asset manager or an institutional investor (pension fund, university endowment, sovereign wealth). Quantitative majors from non-target STEM schools (State schools with strong math or physics programmes, engineering schools, Kaggle-ranked data scientists) are well-regarded for Systematic, Aladdin Engineering, and BlackRock Technology. Your CV must compensate for the non-target signal with a visible, specific, quantified investment-research or quantitative spike — a named portfolio, a published note, a competition win, or a Kaggle placement.

faq.question: What are the most common reasons BlackRock rejects a resume?
faq.answer: (1) Missing CFA progression on Fundamental Equities, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset, or Alternatives applications — screeners expect at least Level 1 candidate. (2) Missing investment-research signal — no published note, no student-managed-investment-fund role, no stock-pitch competition, no investment-club PM signal. (3) Generic 'finance' framing when the role is Fundamental vs Systematic vs Index vs Alternatives vs Aladdin — each has distinct vocabulary. (4) Two-page resume at Analyst or Associate level — automatic screen reject. (5) Missing Aladdin or comparable-platform fluency on investment-platform applications. (6) Missing language proficiency for non-US office applications. (7) Additional section with generic skills ('Microsoft Office', 'teamwork') instead of named fluencies (Aladdin, FactSet, Bloomberg, Python, R, SQL, VBA).
